header.description: Full Stack Developer in Pamplona, Spain 🇪🇸. Passionate about technology, design, and continuous innovation. Committed to creating exceptional web experiences and efficient solutions.

items.description: Full-Stack intern participating in the clinical trials platform by developing APIs in Django, interfaces in React, and managing relational SQL databases.
Organization and receipt of merchandise, inventory control, sorting of mobile technology parts, packaging, and warehouse support.
Customer service, team leadership, task organization, ordering, supplier contact, stock control, and production following guided processes.
Stock control, order management, customer service, billing, deliveries, decision-making advice, and knowledge of IT and office products.

eduItems.title: Higher Degree in Web Application Development (DAW)
eduItems.description: Study of web page design and development, database configuration, frontend frameworks, backend frameworks, and application development.

eduItems.title: Higher Degree in Administration and Finance
eduItems.description: Accounting management, taxation, treasury, financial documentation, and human resources.

items.longDesc: A console-developed system for managing customer relationships. It uses JDBC to communicate with relational databases, implementing CRUD operations and exception handling, alongside flat file backups for local security persistence.
Design and layout of a complete personal website with interconnected pages for CV, About Me, Projects, and Contact. Fully optimized for mobile and tablet viewing using custom media queries.

An exhaustive architectural analysis for a competitive video game tournament platform. It includes class diagrams, use case diagrams, and detailed specification of control flow applying the Model-View-Controller design pattern.

about.title: About Me
about.p1: I was born in Cádiz in 1996. Since I was a child, I've had a great curiosity for the digital world, technology, and cinema—a passion that inspires me to imagine and create.
about.p2: In 2018, I moved to London. This stage was key to my personal growth, helping me face challenges and master English while working in logistics and team leadership.
about.p3: In August 2025, I moved to Pamplona with my wife and her family, where I currently reside and started my professional career in the technology sector. I am focused on continuous learning and teamwork.

interests.title: Interests
interests.intro: I am highly passionate about cinema, a hobby that not only helps me disconnect but also deeply inspires me in my daily life as a developer. I find it fascinating to discover parallels between great big-screen stories and the logic of code.

movies.title: Inception (2010)
movies.quote: To build entire realities from scratch using solid ideas.
movies.relation: Reflection: Like programming: we create worlds and logic out of nothing.

movies.title: The Matrix (1999)
movies.quote: Do not try and bend the spoon, that's impossible. Instead, only try to realize the truth: there is no spoon.
movies.relation: Reflection: Understanding code and the abstraction behind the visual.

movies.title: Good Will Hunting (1997)
movies.quote: Do you like coffee? How about we solve problems together?
movies.relation: Reflection: The passion for dissecting and solving complex logical problems.

movies.title: Interstellar (2014)
movies.quote: Love is the one thing we're capable of perceiving that transcends dimensions of time and space.
movies.relation: Reflection: Persevering through difficult bugs, looking for solutions beyond the obvious.

movies.title: The Social Network (2010)
movies.quote: If you guys were the inventors of Facebook, you'd have invented Facebook.
movies.relation: Reflection: The power of transforming creative ideas into functional code.

movies.title: The Truman Show (1998)
movies.quote: Good morning... and in case I don't see ya: good afternoon, good evening, and good night.
movies.relation: Reflection: Stepping out of your comfort zone and carving your own paths.

movies.title: Whiplash (2014)
movies.quote: There are no two words in the English language more harmful than 'good job'.
movies.relation: Reflection: The constant pursuit of excellence and polishing every detail.

movies.title: The Dark Knight (2008)
movies.quote: You either die a hero, or you live long enough to see yourself become the villain.
movies.relation: Reflection: Refactoring on time: don't let your clean code become unreadable.

movies.title: Back to the Future (1985)
movies.quote: Roads? Where we're going, we don't need roads.
movies.relation: Reflection: Innovating and creating solutions that open up new horizons.

movies.title: The Lord of the Rings (2001)
movies.quote: All we have to decide is what to do with the time that is given to us.
movies.relation: Reflection: Choosing the right tools to leave a lasting impact.

movies.title: A Beautiful Mind (2001)
movies.quote: I have looked through the physical, the metaphysical, the delusional... and back.
movies.relation: Reflection: The constant resolution of abstract logical and mathematical problems.

movies.title: Spider-Man (2002)
movies.quote: With great power comes great responsibility.
movies.relation: Reflection: The responsibility of managing servers and user databases.

movies.title: Blade Runner (1982)
movies.quote: I've seen things you people wouldn't believe...
movies.relation: Reflection: Facing mysterious production bugs that disappear upon restarting.

movies.title: Fight Club (1999)
movies.quote: The first rule of Fight Club is: you do not talk about Fight Club.
movies.relation: Reflection: The first rule of clean code: it should be readable without redundant comments.

movies.title: Jurassic Park (1993)
movies.quote: We spared no expense.
movies.relation: Reflection: When you install 200MB of npm libraries just to fetch data or center a div.

movies.title: The Lion King (1994)
movies.quote: Hakuna Matata. It means no worries.
movies.relation: Reflection: Pushing to production late on a Friday and leaving the office with optimism.

movies.title: Gladiator (2000)
movies.quote: Are you not entertained? Is this not why you are here?
movies.relation: Reflection: Showing the final product demo to the client and seeing everything work flawlessly.

movies.title: The Prestige (2006)
movies.quote: The trick consists of three parts: the pledge, the turn, and the prestige.
movies.relation: Reflection: Creating smooth transitions and micro-animations to hide slow API calls.

movies.title: Pulp Fiction (1994)
movies.quote: Say 'what' again. I dare you, I double dare you.
movies.relation: Reflection: When a compiler gives you the exact same error for the tenth time in a row.

movies.title: Star Wars (1977)
movies.quote: May the Force be with you.
movies.relation: Reflection: May the compiler be on your side and the production build not fail on the first try.

movies.title: Forrest Gump (1994)
movies.quote: Life is like a box of chocolates, you never know what you're gonna get.
movies.relation: Reflection: Taking over a legacy repository without any kind of documentation.

movies.title: Arrival (2016)
movies.quote: Language is the first weapon drawn in a conflict.
movies.relation: Reflection: Learning a completely new programming paradigm or switching languages.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Do you have any proposals or want to know more about me?
contact.successDesc: Thanks for writing. I will reply to you as soon as possible.
contact.errorDesc: The message could not be sent. Please try again later.
